Title: Create configurable bike product with single optional radio section
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Catalog module active. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as ${ADMIN}. 2. Go to Catalog > Products > Add new product. 3. Enter product name: Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 4. Set SKU: BIKE-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. 5. Set base List Price to 200.00 USD. 6. Set inventory to 20. 7. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 8. Add Section: name=Choose Upgrade, type=Radio, optional. 9. Add option: None, $0, qty 20. 10. Add option: Basic Seat, $15, qty 10. 11. Add option: Premium Seat, $45, qty 5. 12. Add option: Racing Seat, $95, qty 2. 13. Publish and save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 14. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL} and open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 15. Verify Configure the parameters widget is visible. 16. Verify section Choose Upgrade shows all 4 options as radio buttons. 17. Verify None is selected by default. Price = $200.00. 18. Select Basic Seat. Verify price = $215.00. 19. Select Premium Seat. Verify price = $245.00. 20. Select Racing Seat. Verify price = $295.00. 21. Return to None. Verify price = $200.00.
Expected Results: Product created with 4 options. Configure the parameters widget appears on PDP. All 4 options present and selectable. Price updates correctly: None=$200 / Basic Seat=$215 / Premium Seat=$245 / Racing Seat=$295. Default selection is None at $200.

Title: Verify optional section allows add-to-cart without selecting an upgrade
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Product Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] published at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Navigate to PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}. 2. Confirm None is selected by default in Choose Upgrade. 3. Do not change selection. 4. Set quantity to 1. 5. Click Add to Cart. 6. Verify cart adds item with no validation error. 7. Open cart. Verify item name and price $200.00. 8. Verify configuration shown as None.
Expected Results: Optional section allows add-to-cart with None selected. No validation error shown. Cart shows product at $200.00 with None option noted.

Title: Verify selected upgrade option is reflected in cart
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Product Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] published at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Navigate to PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}. 2. Select Racing Seat in Choose Upgrade section. 3. Verify price = $295.00. 4. Set quantity to 1. 5. Click Add to Cart. 6. Navigate to cart. 7. Verify item name Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] at $295.00. 8. Verify configuration shows Racing Seat.
Expected Results: Cart shows product with Racing Seat selected at $295.00. Configuration label visible in cart line item.

Title: Verify product configuration widget renders correctly after page refresh
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Navigate to PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Confirm widget loads with 4 options. 3. Select Premium Seat. Verify price = $245.00. 4. Reload the page (F5). 5. Verify widget still visible. 6. Verify section Choose Upgrade shows 4 options. 7. Verify default selection reset to None or retained. 8. Select Basic Seat. Verify price = $215.00.

Title: Verify configuration widget is not shown for a standard non-configurable product
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. A standard product without configuration sections exists.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open or create a simple product with no configuration sections. 2. Confirm no sections defined. 3. Confirm product is published. FRONTEND STEPS: 4. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL} and open PDP for the standard product. 5. Verify NO Configure the parameters widget is shown. 6. Verify Add to Cart button is visible and functional.
Expected Results: Standard non-configurable products do not show the configuration widget. Widget only appears for products with at least one section defined.

Title: Create laptop product with two required radio sections
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Go to Catalog > Products > Add new product. 2. Name: Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: LAPTOP-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. 3. Base price: $999.00. Inventory: 50. 4. Add section: RAM, Radio, Required. Options: 8GB $0 qty 50; 16GB $100 qty 30; 32GB $250 qty 10. 5. Add section: Storage, Radio, Required. Options: 256GB SSD $0 qty 50; 512GB SSD $75 qty 25; 1TB SSD $150 qty 15. 6. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 7. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. 8. Verify both sections RAM and Storage are visible and marked Required. 9. Select RAM=16GB. Verify price=$1,099.00. 10. Select Storage=512GB SSD. Verify price=$1,174.00. 11. Change RAM=32GB. Verify price=$1,324.00. 12. Change Storage=1TB SSD. Verify price=$1,399.00.
Expected Results: Both required sections visible with all options. Price math correct: base $999 + RAM option + Storage option. 16GB+512GB=$1174; 32GB+512GB=$1324; 32GB+1TB=$1399.

Title: Verify required sections block add-to-cart when unselected
Preconditions: TC-E2E-006 completed. Product Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD] published at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Do not select any option. 3. Click Add to Cart. 4. Verify validation error shown. 5. Select RAM=8GB only. Click Add to Cart. 6. Verify still blocked (Storage still unselected). 7. Select Storage=256GB SSD. 8. Click Add to Cart. 9. Verify item added successfully.
Expected Results: Required sections must both be selected before add-to-cart. Validation error shown for incomplete selections. Product adds to cart when both sections have selections.

Title: Verify price math for all section combinations matches admin-set prices
Preconditions: TC-E2E-006 completed. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: Verify option prices in admin: RAM 8GB=$0; 16GB=$100; 32GB=$250. Storage 256GB=$0; 512GB=$75; 1TB=$150. FRONTEND STEPS: Test all 9 combinations: 8GB+256GB=$999; 8GB+512GB=$1074; 8GB+1TB=$1149; 16GB+256GB=$1099; 16GB+512GB=$1174; 16GB+1TB=$1249; 32GB+256GB=$1249; 32GB+512GB=$1324; 32GB+1TB=$1399.
Expected Results: All 9 combinations show correct prices. Price = base $999 + RAM option + Storage option.

Title: Verify multi-section configuration is preserved in cart
Preconditions: TC-E2E-006 completed. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Select RAM=32GB and Storage=1TB SSD. 3. Verify price=$1,399.00. 4. Click Add to Cart. 5. Navigate to cart. 6. Verify price=$1,399.00. 7. Verify both selections shown: RAM=32GB; Storage=1TB SSD.
Expected Results: Both options preserved in cart. Price $1,399.00 correct. Configuration details visible in cart line item.

Title: Verify section order on frontend matches admin configuration order
Preconditions: TC-E2E-006 completed. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Confirm admin shows RAM before Storage. FRONTEND STEPS: 3. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. 4. Verify first section in widget is RAM. 5. Verify second section is Storage.
Expected Results: Sections appear on frontend in same order as configured in Admin. RAM first, Storage second.

Title: Create configurable product with sale price option and verify strikethrough pricing
Section: Scenario 3 - Sale Price vs List Price
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Storefront configured to display both list price and sale price.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Create product: Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: BIKE-SALE-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. 2. Base list price: $300.00; sale price: $250.00. Inventory: 30. 3. Add section: Handlebars, Radio, Optional. 4. Add option: Standard, list=$50, sale=$40, qty=30. 5. Add option: Drop Bar, list=$100, sale=$80, qty=20. 6. Add option: None, list=$0, sale=$0, qty=30. 7. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 8. Open PDP for Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 9. Verify base: list $300 struck through; sale $250 shown. 10. Select Standard Handlebars. Verify: sale=$290 ($250+$40); list=$350 struck through ($300+$50). 11. Select Drop Bar. Verify: sale=$330 ($250+$80); list=$400 struck through ($300+$100).
Expected Results: Storefront shows list price with strikethrough and sale price as active price. Price math uses sale prices. Standard: sale=$290/list=$350. Drop Bar: sale=$330/list=$400.

Title: Verify that option sale price takes precedence over option list price in display
Section: Scenario 3 - Sale Price vs List Price
Preconditions: TC-E2E-011 completed. Product Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD] visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Select Standard Handlebars. 3. Verify active/buyable price = $290.00 (sale), not $350.00 (list). 4. Verify $350 struck through. 5. Select Drop Bar. 6. Verify active price = $330.00 (sale), not $400.00 (list). 7. Verify $400 struck through.
Expected Results: Sale prices always shown as active price. List prices shown crossed out. Applies to both base price and option-adjusted totals.

Title: Verify sale price options are reflected correctly in cart total
Section: Scenario 3 - Sale Price vs List Price
Preconditions: TC-E2E-011 completed. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Sale Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Select Drop Bar. Verify sale price = $330.00. 3. Click Add to Cart. 4. Navigate to cart. 5. Verify line item shows $330.00 (sale price). 6. Verify cart total reflects sale price.
Expected Results: Cart uses sale price $330.00. List price $400.00 not used in cart calculations.

Title: Create configurable product with an out-of-stock option and verify it is blocked on frontend
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Create product: Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: BIKE-OOS-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. Base price: $500. Inventory: 50. 2. Add section: Frame Color, Radio, Required. 3. Add option: Red, $0, qty=10. Add option: Blue, $0, qty=5. Add option: Limited Edition Black, $50, qty=0. Add option: Silver, $25, qty=8. 4. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 5. Open PDP for Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 6. Verify all 4 options shown in Frame Color section. 7. Verify Red, Blue, Silver are selectable. 8. Verify Limited Edition Black is disabled/greyed out. 9. Attempt to click Limited Edition Black. Verify it cannot be selected. 10. Select Silver. Verify price=$525.00. 11. Click Add to Cart. Verify success.
Expected Results: Out-of-stock option (qty=0) visible but not selectable. In-stock options remain selectable. Product addable to cart with in-stock options.

Title: Verify out-of-stock option label or indicator is displayed to the user
Preconditions: TC-E2E-014 completed. Product Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD] visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Locate Limited Edition Black option. 3. Verify it is visually distinct (greyed out / Out of Stock label / strikethrough). 4. Verify tooltip or label indicates unavailability. 5. Verify in-stock options have no such indicator.
Expected Results: Out-of-stock options clearly visually differentiated. Users can identify why option cannot be selected.

Title: Verify required section with all options out-of-stock blocks add-to-cart entirely
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Create product: Test Config AllOOS [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: CFG-ALLAOOS-[YYYYMMDD]. Base price: $100. Inventory: 0. 2. Add section: Material, Radio, Required. 3. Add option: Carbon, $0, qty=0. Add option: Titanium, $50, qty=0. 4. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 5. Open PDP for Test Config AllOOS [YYYYMMDD]. 6. Verify Material section shows both options disabled. 7. Verify Add to Cart is hidden/disabled or shows Out of Stock. 8. Attempt to click Add to Cart. Verify no item added.
Expected Results: When all options in a required section are out of stock, product is not purchasable. Add to Cart disabled/hidden.

Title: Complete purchase flow — Admin create
Preconditions: Critical
Steps: 25 min
column11: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Registered customer account exists at ${FRONT_URL}. Checkout and payment configured in QA environment.
column12: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Create product: Test Config Checkout Bike [YYYYMMDD]. SKU: BIKE-CHK-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]. Base price: $150. Inventory: 25. 2. Add section: Wheels, Radio, Required. Options: Standard Wheels $0 qty 25; Sport Wheels $50 qty 10. 3. Publish and save. FRONTEND STEPS: 4. Sign in at ${FRONT_URL}. 5. Open PDP for Test Config Checkout Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 6. Select Sport Wheels. Verify price=$200.00. 7. Add to Cart. 8. Navigate to cart. Verify item at $200.00 with Sport Wheels. 9. Proceed to Checkout. 10. Verify Sport Wheels shown in checkout line item at $200.00. 11. Complete checkout with test payment. 12. Verify order confirmation page and order number. 13. Navigate to Account > Order History. 14. Verify order with correct total.
column13: Full purchase flow works for configurable product. Sport Wheels selection preserved through cart, checkout, and order history. Order total = $200.00.

Title: Verify order history shows configured option details
Preconditions: TC-E2E-017 completed. Order visible in admin and customer order history.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Orders. 2. Open order from TC-E2E-017. 3. Verify: product=Test Config Checkout Bike [YYYYMMDD]; config=Sport Wheels; price=$200.00. FRONTEND STEPS: 4. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL} > Account > Order History. 5. Open the order. 6. Verify product name. 7. Verify Sport Wheels shown in line item. 8. Verify price=$200.00.
Expected Results: Both admin and customer order history show configured option alongside product. Prices consistent.

Title: Verify multiple configurable products in one checkout
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 and TC-E2E-006 completed. Both products visible at ${FRONT_URL}. Registered customer account exists.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Sign in at ${FRONT_URL}. 2. Open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Select Premium Seat. Add to Cart. 3. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. Select RAM=16GB and Storage=512GB SSD. Add to Cart. 4. Navigate to cart. Verify: Bike at $245.00 (Premium Seat); Laptop at $1,174.00 (16GB+512GB). Subtotal=$1,419.00. 5. Proceed through checkout and place order. 6. Verify both items in order confirmation with correct configs and prices.
Expected Results: Multiple configurable products checkout successfully. Each configuration preserved independently. Cart and order totals correct.

Title: Verify cart quantity update for configurable product
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Product Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. 2. Select Basic Seat. Verify price=$215.00. 3. Set quantity to 3. 4. Add to Cart. 5. In cart: verify qty=3; config=Basic Seat; line total=$645.00. 6. Update cart qty to 2. 7. Verify line total=$430.00. 8. Verify Basic Seat still shown.
Expected Results: Quantity updates correctly recalculate line total. Configuration remains associated after quantity change.

Title: Edit option price in Admin and verify updated price on frontend
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] in Catalog > Products. 2. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 3. Find Premium Seat option in Choose Upgrade section. 4. Edit price: change from $45.00 to $60.00. Save option. 5. Save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 6. Open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}. Hard reload. 7. Select Premium Seat. Verify price=$260.00 ($200+$60), NOT old $245.00. 8. Select Basic Seat. Verify price=$215.00 (unchanged). 9. Select Racing Seat. Verify price=$295.00 (unchanged).
Expected Results: Updated price ($60) reflected on frontend for Premium Seat. New total = $260.00 instead of old $245.00. Other options unaffected.

Title: Verify cart reflects updated option price after admin edit
Preconditions: TC-E2E-021 completed. Premium Seat price is now $60.00.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: None required. FRONTEND STEPS: 1. Open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}. 2. Select Premium Seat. Verify price=$260.00. 3. Click Add to Cart. 4. Navigate to cart. 5. Verify line item price=$260.00. 6. Verify line item shows Premium Seat.
Expected Results: Cart uses updated price $260.00 for Premium Seat. Old price $245.00 not used.

Title: Edit option name in Admin and verify updated name on frontend
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 2. Find Basic Seat option. 3. Edit name: change to Standard Seat. Save. 4. Save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 5. Open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Hard reload. 6. Verify Basic Seat no longer shown. 7. Verify Standard Seat now appears. 8. Select Standard Seat. Verify price=$215.00 (unchanged). 9. Verify other options remain: None, Premium Seat, Racing Seat.
Expected Results: Renamed option Standard Seat appears on frontend instead of Basic Seat. Price unchanged. Other options unaffected.

Title: Delete an option in Admin and verify it disappears from frontend
Preconditions: TC-E2E-001 completed. Product has 4 options. Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}. Product visible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 2. Find Racing Seat option. 3. Delete Racing Seat. Confirm deletion. 4. Verify section shows 3 options: None, Basic/Standard Seat, Premium Seat. 5. Save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 6. Open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Hard reload. 7. Verify only 3 options shown in Choose Upgrade. 8. Verify Racing Seat NOT present. 9. Verify remaining options (None, Basic/Standard Seat, Premium Seat) present. 10. Select Premium Seat. Verify price correct.
Expected Results: Deleted option Racing Seat no longer shown on frontend. Remaining options functional. Price calculation works with remaining options.

Title: Delete an entire configuration section in Admin and verify widget updates on frontend
Preconditions: TC-E2E-006 completed. Product Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD] has two sections: RAM and Storage. Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 2. Find Storage section. 3. Delete entire Storage section. Confirm deletion. 4. Verify only RAM section remains. 5. Save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 6. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. Hard reload. 7. Verify widget shows only one section: RAM. 8. Verify Storage section completely gone. 9. Verify RAM options (8GB, 16GB, 32GB) present. 10. Select 32GB RAM. Verify price=$1,249.00 ($999+$250). No Storage component. 11. Select 8GB RAM. Add to Cart. Verify success at $999.00.
Expected Results: Storage section removed from frontend widget. Only RAM section remains. Price recalculated without Storage. $999+$250=$1249 for 32GB.

Title: Verify deleting the only section removes the configuration widget entirely
Preconditions: TC-E2E-025 completed. Laptop has only RAM section remaining. Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 2. Find RAM section. 3. Delete RAM section. Confirm deletion. 4. Verify no sections remain. 5. Save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 6. Open PDP for Test Config Laptop [YYYYMMDD]. Hard reload. 7. Verify Configure the parameters widget is NOT visible. 8. Verify product treated as standard. 9. Verify base price $999.00 shown. 10. Verify Add to Cart accessible with no configuration required.
Expected Results: When all sections deleted, configuration widget removed from PDP. Product behaves as standard non-configurable product.

Title: Verify deleting a required option when it was the default forces user re-selection
Preconditions: TC-E2E-014 completed. Product Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD] has 4 options including Limited Edition Black (qty=0). Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL}.
Steps: ADMIN STEPS: 1. Open Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab. 2. Find Limited Edition Black option. 3. Delete Limited Edition Black entirely (not just set qty to 0). 4. Verify Frame Color section shows: Red, Blue, Silver (3 options). 5. Save product. FRONTEND STEPS: 6. Open PDP for Test Config OOS Bike [YYYYMMDD]. Hard reload. 7. Verify only 3 options: Red, Blue, Silver. 8. Verify Limited Edition Black NOT present (not even as disabled). 9. Select Blue. Verify price=$500.00. 10. Click Add to Cart. Verify success.
Expected Results: Deleted option completely removed (not shown as disabled). Remaining options enabled and selectable. Product addable to cart with remaining options.
